meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about a missing portfolio. Learn to leverage Git with AI to ship production-ready SaaS MVPs fast. Master Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth; founders are hired by revenue, not resumes. By using Git as your automated documentation engine and Claude Code to accelerate development, you can transform raw commits into a verified, high-velocity technical narrative that proves your competency to investors and early adopters alike.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the SaaS market is that no one cares about your degree or your empty GitHub profile. They care about shipping. If you are stuck in the 'no portfolio' loop, you are losing valuable time that should be spent on customer discovery and revenue validation. The market rewards the fast, not the credentialed.

Today, we stop building 'projects' and start building 'proofs.' By integrating Git workflows with AI-driven development agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can turn every feature push into a documented milestone. This isn't just coding; it's engineering a reputation for velocity that speaks louder than any static website ever could.

steps.title: Architecting the Proof-of-Concept
steps.description: Define the core problem your MVP solves. Don't build everything; build the one feature that makes someone reach for their wallet.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this business goal: [INSERT GOAL]. Generate a lean project architecture focused on speed-to-market and modularity, using a modern stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project scaffolding for [PROJECT_NAME] with structured directory layout'

steps.title: Automating Commit Narratives
steps.description: Your Git commit history is your resume. Use AI to generate semantic commits that describe the 'why' behind the code, not just the 'what'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current changes and generate a conventional commit message that highlights the business value and technical complexity of this feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Draft a commit message for these changes, following conventional commits, focusing on the feature impact.'

steps.title: Rapid Iteration with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to handle the boilerplate, allowing you to focus on high-level logic and user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the logic of this component and suggest three ways to optimize it for better performance and maintainability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the authentication flow to use Supabase, optimizing for security and speed.'

steps.title: Documenting the 'How' via Readme AI
steps.description: A repository without documentation is a graveyard. Automate your README to show potential users how your code solves their pain points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a compelling README.md for this project that emphasizes the problem, the solution, and the technical architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a README.md that includes a quick-start guide and a technical overview.'

steps.title: Validating through Deployment
steps.description: Code only matters when it's live. Push to a production environment to demonstrate that you can manage a full software lifecycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for this project, including CI/CD best practices and environment variable management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure a Vercel deployment pipeline for this project'

pro_tips: Treat your Git history as a public ledger of your decision-making process; it’s more valuable than the code itself.
Use Claude Code to perform 'automated code reviews' on your own PRs before merging to catch anti-patterns.
Tag your releases with version numbers early; it signals to potential stakeholders that you think in terms of product maturity.
Always include a 'Lessons Learned' section in your README to demonstrate technical humility and growth.
Integrate your Git activity with a public-facing dashboard to visualize your progress for non-technical investors.
content.next_steps: Once your first repo is polished, publish a 'Build Log' post on X or LinkedIn linking to the repository, focusing on the business problem you solved rather than the code you wrote.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting a blank profile hold your vision hostage. You have the tools, the AI, and the mandate to ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
